intro: Loxanthes striata, also known as the striated loxanthes, is a species of flowering plant in the family Gentianaceae. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities, which can aid in the treatment of various ailments such as wounds, infections, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Loxanthes striata include gentiopicrin, gentianine, and loganin, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The plant is commonly used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to support immune function, reduce inflammation, and promote overall well-being.
